Screen.TooltipReference: Drop Item Here to Auto Equip

Label.TooltipReference: Hit Points
Mana
Endurance
Mitigation and Evasion components of your Armor Class
Mitigation: Chance to take less melee damage
Mitigation Softcap: Mitigation beyond this value will have diminishing returns
Evasion: Chance to avoid melee damage
Offense and Accuracy components of your melee attacks
Offense: Chance to deal more damage
Accuracy: Chance to land a hit
Shows the speed at which you attack - the base is 100%
Shows the speed at which you are moving
The number of hit points you will regenerate while in combat
The number of mana points you will regenerate while in combat
The number of endurance points you will regenerate while in combat
Damage Per Second of your primary weapon.
Damage Per Second of your secondary weapon.
Damage Per Second of your range weapon.
Determines your encumbrance limit and is a factor in melee damage
Influences your hit point and endurance totals
Determines your mana (for some classes) and influences your chance to raise skills
Determines your mana (for some classes) and influences your chance to raise skills
Influences your AC and helps you avoid incoming damage
Influences chance to hit, damage done, and chance to skill up
Influences pricing on many vendors and influences success of charm and mesmerization spells
Resists magic-based damage spells
Resists fire-based damage spells
Resists cold-based damage spells
Resists disease-based damage spells
Resists poison-based damage spells
Resists void-based damage spells
Increases the rate at which you will trigger proc spells during melee
Reduces the base damage of incoming instant spell attacks
Reduces the base damage of incoming melee attacks
Adds damage to any damage shield effect you have
Reduces the base damage of incoming damage-over-time spell attacks
Reduces the damage you take from damage shield effects on your target
Adds to your ability to avoid incoming melee attacks
Adds to your ability to make a successful melee attack
Increases the chance for you to resist stun effects
Allows your melee attacks to bypass your opponent's active defenses
Adds to the effectiveness of your healing spells
Adds to the effectiveness of your damage spells
Adds a chance to return mana to you on a successful spell cast
Has a chance to improve the effects of Heroic Mods, skill successes, and critical potency
Adds to damage when using the Bash skill
Adds to damage when using the Backstab skill
Adds to damage when using the Dragon Punch skill
Adds to damage when using the Eagle Strike skill
Adds to damage when using the Flying Kick skill
Adds to damage when using the Kick skill
Adds to damage when using the Round Kick skill
Adds to damage when using the Tiger Claw skill
Adds to damage when using the Frenzy skill
Location you return to upon death.
Location you return to when using the Origin Alternate Advancement ability.
Character deity.

Button.Text: Show currencies that you don't have.
Button.TooltipReference: Toggle whether you want to display currencies that you have.
Click here to convert the highlighted point type into a physical item.
Click here to convert your held item or any items of the highlighted point type into points.
Click here to convert all currency items into points.

Listbox.TooltipReference: Drop an exaltation here to add it to the Exaltation Storage, or left click and hold on a selected Exaltation to remove it from the Exaltation Storage.

Button.TooltipReference: Click here to go to the Marketplace to purchase additional Exaltation Storage slots.

Listbox.TooltipReference: Drop an activated item here to add it to the Activated Item Storage, or left click and hold on a selected item to remove it from the Activated Item Storage.

Button.TooltipReference: Click here to go to the Marketplace to purchase additional Activated Item Storage slots.

Listbox.TooltipReference: Drop a piece of equipment here to add it to the Equipment Storage, or left click and hold on a selected item to remove it from the Equipment Storage. Equipment items are items that can be equipped, aren't stackable, and aren't expendable.

Button.TooltipReference: Click here to go to the Marketplace to purchase additional Equipment Storage slots.
When checked, equipment that would normally go into your bags when loading an equipment set will be added to the Equipment Key Ring instead.

Label.TooltipReference: Equipment slots for your pet. The amount of slots available is dependent on your class loadout.
Hit Points
Mana
Endurance
Mitigation and Evasion components of your pet's Armor Class
Mitigation: Chance to take less melee damage
Evasion: Chance to avoid melee damage
Offense and Accuracy components of your pet's melee attacks
Offense: Chance to deal more damage
Accuracy: Chance to land a hit
Shows the speed at which your pet attacks - the base is 100%
The number of hit points your pet will regenerate while in combat
The number of mana points your pet will regenerate while in combat
The number of endurance points your pet will regenerate while in combat
A factor in your pet's melee damage
Influences your pet's hit point and endurance totals
Influences your pet's AC and helps them avoid incoming damage
Influences chance to hit, damage done, and chance to skill up
Determines your pet's mana (for some classes)
Determines your pet's mana (for some classes)
Resists poison-based damage spells
Resists magic-based damage spells
Resists disease-based damage spells
Resists fire-based damage spells
Resists cold-based damage spells
Resists void-based damage spells

Screen.TooltipReference: Drop Item Here to Auto Equip

Label.TooltipReference: Bard
Beastlord
Berserker
Cleric
Bard
Beastlord
Berserker
Cleric
Druid
Enchanter
Magician
Monk
Druid
Enchanter
Magician
Monk
Necromancer
Paladin
Ranger
Rogue
Necromancer
Paladin
Ranger
Rogue
Shadow Knight
Shaman
Warrior
Wizard
Shadow Knight
Shaman
Warrior
Wizard

Button.TooltipReference: Platinum Pieces. 1 Platinum is equal to 10 Gold.
Gold Pieces. 1 Gold is equal to 10 Silver.
Silver Pieces. 1 Silver is equal to 10 Copper.

Label.TooltipReference: The percentage of experience gained towards the next level.
The percentage of experience gained towards the next Alternate Advancement point.
Player weight. Maximum weight is your characters current Strength. Carrying over your maximum weight will lead to encumbrance.
Player weight of all worn equipment.
